body.title: [sprint] Inference router increment 2 — the trivial selector: two bucket-filters, argmin(dollars), failure-bump
body.intention: I intend that per call the Proxy picks the cheapest capable source by a trivial rule over the schema, so cascades and the escalation fork both dissolve into one selector.
body.description:
**Problem:** the candidate sort (rules_engine.py:200-219) keys on a binary `billing_type` — replace it with the cost-optimizing selector over the increment-1 schema. This is increment 2 of D-inference-cost-optimizing-router. GATED on T-router-data-schema (needs the descriptor fields + mappers).
**Proposed shape — one trivial rule, all richness in the data:**
(a) **Filter** to sources whose `time_bucket` is eligible for the call's `eligible_time_bucket` AND whose `difficulty_capable >= ` the call's `difficulty_bucket` AND whose `features` cover the required features.
(b) **argmin(`dollars_per_unit`)** over survivors; priority as the final tiebreak.
(c) **On OBSERVED MECHANICAL failure, bump the call's difficulty bucket one rung and RE-RUN the same selector.** v1 failure = mechanical ONLY (exception / timeout / did-not-reach-DONE); quality-based escalation (a quality judge) is explicitly DEFERRED — do not build it here. This IS capability-escalation — one mechanism, NOT a separate cascade/fork. The a-priori difficulty guess is allowed to be sloppy precisely because this corrects it. **The bump MUST terminate:** bumping past the top bucket (`design`) hard-fails CLEANLY (a terminal routing error + system_alarm), never loops.
**Hex-DOWN is not a branch:** an unavailable source is simply absent from the candidate set -> next-cheapest-capable wins automatically. The down raises a separate `system_alarm` (observability signal), never a routing conditional.
**Retire:** the binary `billing_type` sort; the `foreground` inversion (rules_engine.py:205-212) -> REDEFINE foreground as 'permit a faster, costlier time-bucket for latency' (against time_bucket, not billing); the dead source name `local_ollama` (rules_engine.py:95-96) -> `ollama`.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/rules_engine.py (replace candidate sort with bucket-filter->argmin(dollars); add failure-bump re-run; redefine foreground; local_ollama->ollama).
**Design rules:** ALL inference through the Proxy; NO SQLite; log source/model/buckets/dollars at the crossing (schema from increment 1).
**Scope boundary:** the selector + escalation-by-rerun ONLY. Populating real source numbers (Hex roster) is increment 3 (T-ds-local-ollama-route). Do NOT register models or set INFERENCE_ENDPOINT here. NOTE: this reorders ALL routing (every tier/caller) — blast radius is the whole Proxy.
**Test plan (red->green):** (1) two capable sources differing only in dollars -> cheaper wins; (2) a cheap-but-wrong-time-bucket source is EXCLUDED for an interactive call, INCLUDED for overnight; (3) a source below the difficulty bucket is excluded; (4) failure-bump: a too-weak pick fails MECHANICALLY -> re-run lands on the next difficulty bucket; (5) **bump past the top bucket (`design`) -> clean terminal failure + system_alarm, NO loop**; (6) source-absent (Hex down) -> next-cheapest-capable, no hard-fail; (7) foreground redefined -> token-direct permitted under latency; (8) grep `local_ollama` -> 0 live refs.
**Completion criteria:** candidate selection is bucket-filter->argmin(dollars) with priority tiebreak; escalation is a MECHANICAL-failure difficulty-bump re-run of the SAME selector (quality-judge deferred); the bump TERMINATES cleanly past the top bucket (no loop); foreground redefined against time-bucket; local_ollama gone; all eight tests green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
